admin.heading: Client management
admin.description: Enable or disable the modules for each client. Overview and Settings are always enabled.

campanhas.title: Campaigns
campanhas.subtitle: Bulk sending with scheduling, A/B testing and anti-detection (jitter + rate limit + daily cap).
campanhas.emptyDescription: Create your first campaign to send messages in bulk.
campanhas.antiDetectionHint: With {interval}s ± {jitter}s between sends, WhatsApp won't detect a pattern. A cap of {cap}/day avoids bans.
campanhas.abTestDesc: Splits the audience between 2 variants to compare performance

fields.objective: Objective (for AI to generate the message)
fields.objectivePlaceholder: e.g. Re-engage customers who abandoned the cart in the last 7 days
fields.createNewTemplate: — create a new template from the text below —

confirm.launchMessage: Launch campaign "{name}"? Sends will be scheduled immediately.
confirm.deleteMessage: Delete campaign "{name}"? Targets will also be removed.

amazon.description: Manage listings and orders via the Amazon Seller Central SP-API.
amazon.feature1: Import Seller Central listings into the eClick catalog.
amazon.feature2: Real-time stock and price sync via SP-API.
amazon.feature3: Sales, Buy Box, and per-ASIN performance reports.
amazon.feature4: Manage FBA and FBM directly from the dashboard.

magalu.description: Publish and manage listings on the Magazine Luiza Marketplace.
magalu.feature1: Create and update products via the Magalu Marketplace API.
magalu.feature2: Sync stock and prices with the internal catalog.

shopee.description: Sync listings, orders, and stock with Shopee.
shopee.feature1: Import and create listings via the Shopee Open Platform API.
shopee.feature2: Automatic stock sync between Shopee and the internal catalog.
shopee.feature3: Manage orders, logistics, and seller vouchers.

syncs.title: Syncs
syncs.subtitle: History of stock syncs with platforms

stock.title: Stock
stock.legend: Physical = warehouse stock · Virtual = extra quantity for the platform · Platform = total listed · Min. stock = click to edit · Auto-pause = pause listing when minimum is reached

drawer.noDistribution: No distribution configured — the available stock is published in full to every linked channel.

links.title: Link Management
links.subtitle: Connect products to listings on any platform and set up kits and variations

modal.title: Link Listing
modal.invalidId: Invalid ID — use the MLB1234567 format or paste the listing URL

stock.tooltipVirtual: Extra quantity shown on the platforms beyond the real physical stock. Useful for Shopee Flash Sale and campaigns that require a high minimum stock to qualify.
stock.tooltipMinPause: When (physical + virtual) reaches this value, the listing is paused automatically. Example: physical=5, virtual=1,000, minimum=1,000 → pauses when physical hits zero (total drops from 1,005 to 1,000).
stock.title: Stock
stock.invalidChannel: Invalid channel: "{channel}" — use the slug (e.g. mercadolivre)
stock.emptyDistText: Define how much of the available stock goes to each marketplace. Without configuration, the system sends the full amount to every linked channel.
stock.channelNotIntegratedHint: Channel not integrated — distribution saved but sync disabled.
stock.missingSalesData: Channels with no sales in 30d (get only the 10% floor):
stock.useModeMeanwhile: Use <pct>Percentage</pct> or <fix>Fixed</fix> mode in the meantime.
stock.basedOnSales: Based on sales from the last 30 days, with a 10% floor per channel:

icarus.title: Icarus Integration (Pennacorp)
icarus.subtitle: Syncs the supplier's catalog and stock. Their <sale>selling price</sale> minus your discount becomes your <cost>cost</cost>.
icarus.disconnectConfirm: Disconnect Icarus? The token will be deactivated but the sync history is kept.
icarus.howToGetToken: How to get the access token?

tokenHelp.request: The supplier must formally request it from Pennacorp via an <highlight>API Usage Request</highlight>.

form.accessTokenHint: Paste the token received from Pennacorp
form.accessTokenPlaceholder: Paste the long access_token generated by Pennacorp here…
form.baseUrlHint: Leave empty if the supplier uses Pennacorp's default environment
form.ecommOnly: Sync only products active in the supplier's e-commerce
form.notesPlaceholder: e.g. technical contact, notes about the token…

discount.title: Cinderella general discount
discount.subtitle: Applies to all synced products that don't have their own adjustment.

catalog.title: Catalog sync
catalog.subtitle: Pulls the Pennacorp catalog and shows what's already synced. Select and sync only what you need.
catalog.pullSlow: Pulling the catalog is taking longer than expected. Reload the page in a few minutes.
catalog.empty: Empty catalog — click <highlight>Pull catalog</highlight> to load it from Pennacorp.

linked.title: Linked products
linked.subtitle: Cost = Cinderella price minus the adjustment. With no own adjustment, it uses the general discount.
linked.noResults: No product found for the search.
linked.empty: No product linked yet — sync items in the catalog above.

errors.htmlResponse: The Pennacorp server returned an error page instead of the API. Check the token and leave the "Base URL" field blank.
errors.pasteToken: Paste the access token received from Pennacorp.

fields.name: Name

step.offsetFromHint: Anchor event used to compute the delay (delivered = N time after delivery).

immediate.label: Immediate
immediate.hint: Fires as soon as the journey starts.

status_change_ml.label: Status change (ML)
status_change_ml.hint: Waits for the order status to change. Use a condition to filter (e.g. shipping_status=shipped).

time_offset.label: After delay/time
time_offset.hint: Waits N time after something (offset_from). Use delay + offset_from.

confirm.deactivateMessage: Deactivate template? Historical runs are preserved.

templates.previewHint: Known variables use mock values; unknown ones appear in <red>red</red>.

autoComm.title: Automated Communication
autoComm.descOn: Every new sale automatically triggers the post-sale journey (welcome → tracking → delivery).
autoComm.descOff: Nothing is sent automatically. Messages only go out via manual action.

rules.title: Sending rules
rules.pause: Pause customer with no reply after

errors.windowEnd: End time must be after the start time

toast.noSettings: No settings found for this org

geral.title: General
geral.subtitle: Your account and organization information.
geral.name: Name
geral.securityNote: Authentication managed via Supabase Auth. Change your password directly through the reset link sent by email.

equipe.title: Team
equipe.removeMemberMessage: Remove this member from the organization?
equipe.endpointNotConfiguredShort: The /api/teams/invite endpoint is not configured yet.
equipe.endpointNotConfigured: The /api/teams/invite endpoint is not configured yet. Add members via Supabase Dashboard → Authentication → Users.
equipe.inviteHint: The invitation is sent via Supabase Auth (magic link). If the endpoint isn't configured yet, add users directly in Supabase Dashboard → Authentication → Users.

preferencias.title: Preferences
preferencias.subtitle: Privacy, notifications, appearance and account data
preferencias.placeholderNotificacoes: Email / Slack / WhatsApp triggers not implemented yet.
preferencias.placeholderConta: Email, password, active sessions, export data.
preferencias.maskingDesc: How tax ID, phone and email appear in tables (customers, orders, etc). The full data stays available by clicking the eye icon.
preferencias.exportDesc: How personal data appears in exported CSV. CSV is offline — usually useful in plain text, but you can force masking here.
preferencias.maskExport: Mask personal data when exporting CSV
preferencias.exportPreviewMasked: CSV will have tax IDs/phones/emails masked
preferencias.exportPreviewFull: CSV will have full tax IDs/phones/emails
preferencias.sessionDesc: When you click the eye, how long the full data stays visible before being masked again.
preferencias.auditDesc: Every time you click the eye to see a full tax ID/phone/email, we log it here (data protection).

aggregator.title: Sales Aggregator
aggregator.subtitle: Syncs orders from the Mercado Livre API and aggregates metrics by product/day.
aggregator.cancelRunDesc: This will mark the run as cancelled. The processing in progress will continue until the current day is finished.
aggregator.startBackfillDesc: This will import orders from the last {days} days. The operation may take over 30 minutes. Continue?

ia.title: Artificial Intelligence
ia.subtitle: API keys, models per feature, usage and costs
ia.featuresHint: Each feature uses the model configured below. If the primary fails with a 5xx error or timeout, the fallback takes over automatically.
ia.configureFallback: Configure the fallback or turn off the toggle
ia.fallbackHint: — takes over automatically if the primary fails
ia.costPerDayChart: Cost per day (USD), stacked by feature
ia.howStep1: Each AI feature (campaign copy, assistant, embeddings, etc) uses a configured model on this screen.
ia.howStep2: If you don't configure anything, the feature uses the system default — chosen by our team.
ia.howStep3: If the primary model fails with a server error (5xx) or timeout, the system automatically tries the fallback.
ia.howStep4: Every call is logged in ai_usage_log with tokens, cost and a fallback flag. See it on the Usage tab.
ia.keysSecurityNote: Keys are never sent to the frontend. Every AI call goes through the backend, which uses the encrypted keys stored in api_credentials.
ia.imagesHint: The campaign cover feature uses the provider configured below to generate images. Currently only OpenAI gpt-image-1 is available — Flux Pro and other providers are coming soon.
ia.featureNotFound: Feature campaign_card not found — the backend may be outdated.
ia.canvaConnectionDesc: Lets you open generated covers directly in the Canva editor to refine them manually.
ia.canvaUnavailable: Canva not available on this plan. Coming soon.
ia.costsImagesNote: Only campaign_card. To see costs for all features, open the Usage tab.

whatsappRoutes.title: WhatsApp Routes
whatsappRoutes.subtitle: Configure which WhatsApp channel sends each type of message. Without an explicit route, the system chooses automatically based on the category (internal → free Baileys · customer → official Cloud API).
whatsappRoutes.clearRouteMessage: Return "{title}" to automatic fallback? The system will choose an available channel based on the category.
whatsappRoutes.noChannelsDesc: You need to connect at least one channel before configuring routes. You have 2 options:
whatsappRoutes.purpose_internal_alert_desc: AI alerts from Intelligence Hub, ads-ai and pricing for the internal team
whatsappRoutes.purpose_internal_alert_example: Imminent SKU stockout · low ROAS · critical margin · daily digest
whatsappRoutes.purpose_manager_verification_desc: 6-digit WA code when you register a new IH manager
whatsappRoutes.purpose_customer_journey_desc: Automatic messages based on order status (shipped, delivered, etc)
whatsappRoutes.purpose_customer_journey_example: Order shipped · Order delivered · NPS survey
whatsappRoutes.purpose_customer_campaign_desc: Mass broadcasts to customers (VIP segments, churn, new launches)
whatsappRoutes.noFallbackChannel: No channel available for fallback. Connect a channel above.
whatsappRoutes.helpBaileys: Baileys is free (generates a QR on the channel) but unofficial — risk of a ban if used for spam. Good for low-volume internal alerts.
whatsappRoutes.helpCloudApi: Cloud API is official Meta (paid, ~R$ 0.10-0.80/message) — green tier, approved templates, scales well for mass marketing.
whatsappRoutes.helpFallback: Without an explicit route, the system uses a fallback based on the purpose category.

whatsapp.title: WhatsApp
whatsapp.description: WhatsApp Business integration for support, order notifications and re-engagement campaigns.

customerHub.title: Customer Hub
customerHub.subtitle: RFM · ABC curve · Segmentation · Churn risk. A daily cron at 03:17 BRT recalculates everything automatically.

overview.intro: Consolidated view of RFM, ABC and churn. Manual recompute below (the daily cron at 03:17 BRT also runs).

abc.intro: Pareto distribution + top 200 by revenue table. Export CSV for targeted actions.

rfm.noData: No RFM data. Run "Recompute metrics" on the Overview.

rCard.name: Recency
rCard.desc: How many days since the last purchase. Lower = better (engaged customer).

fCard.name: Frequency
fCard.desc: How many purchases the customer made. More purchases = better (recurring customer).

mCard.name: Monetary
mCard.desc: Total spent so far. Higher = better (high-value customer).

rfm.mapDesc: X = Frequency · Y = Recency (lower at top) · size = Monetary · color = score (green 7-10, yellow 4-7, red 0-4)

churn.intro: Bucketed by days since last purchase. A daily cron recalculates. Use the re-engagement campaign to win back high/critical.
churn.tableTitle: Customers at high/critical risk — top 200 by LTV

segments.intro: Segments with dynamic rules. A daily cron recalculates auto_refresh=true.
segments.empty: No segments yet. Create one to use in Messaging campaigns.

confirm.deleteMessage: Delete segment? Members will be removed (customers preserved).

toast.noCustomersToExport: Segment has no customers to export

onboarding.criticalDesc: Complete the steps below to start dropship operations

step1.title: Register your first dropship partner
step1.desc: Supplier that ships directly to the buyer

step2.title: Link a marketplace account to the partner
step2.desc: Which ML/Shopee/Amazon orders will be dropship

step3.title: Set up an email provider
step3.desc: Required to send POs to the partner (Resend/SendGrid)

accountSuppliers.title: Account ↔ Partner Link
accountSuppliers.subtitle: Map which partner ships each marketplace account's orders

explainer.title: How it works
explainer.text: When an order comes in from a marketplace account (ML/Shopee/Amazon), the system looks up the partner linked in this table and creates the dropship identification with the correct supplier. To unlink, we use active_until (not DELETE) — old orders keep their historical reference.

form.accountsCount: {count, plural, one {# connected account} other {# connected accounts}}. Those shown in red already have an active partner.

unlinkConfirm.title: Unlink account?
unlinkConfirm.message: The history of old orders is kept, but new orders will NO longer be assigned to this partner.

errors.provideAccountId: Provide at least one marketplace account ID

copilot.title: Dropship Copilot
copilot.subtitle: Ask questions about partners, POs, returns, discrepancies
copilot.helpDescription: I have access to the dashboard KPIs, partner scores, returns and discrepancies.
copilot.footer: v1: free-text answers · context includes dashboard + scores + returns + critical discrepancies

suggestions.s1: Which partners are at risk today?
suggestions.s2: How much do I have to pay in the next 7 days?
suggestions.s4: What critical discrepancies do I have?
suggestions.s5: How is the top partner's score?
suggestions.s6: Which SKUs are out of stock?

credits.title: Partner Credits
credits.subtitle: Balance of return credits to offset against the partner's next PO
credits.partnersWithBalance: {count, plural, one {# partner with balance} other {# partners with balance}}

disputes.title: Disputes
disputes.subtitle: Conflicts over cost, responsibility or amounts between seller and partner

newModal.title: Open Dispute
newModal.openerName: Name of who is opening it (optional)
newModal.reasonPlaceholder: e.g. Cost of SKU ABC-123 is $5 higher than agreed

resolveModal.title: Resolve Dispute
resolveModal.resolutionPlaceholder: Detail the decision (goes to the audit record)

divergences.title: Discrepancies
divergences.subtitle: Automatic detection daily at 2 AM · 3 v1 rules (shipping delay, mapping, price < cost)

empty.title: No discrepancies in this filter
empty.hint: All good. Or maybe a scan hasn't run yet — click "Scan now".

resolvePrompt.title: Resolve discrepancy
resolvePrompt.message: Describe how it was resolved (goes to the audit record).
resolvePrompt.placeholder: e.g. Partner confirmed shipping at 4 PM after contact

ignorePrompt.title: Ignore discrepancy
ignorePrompt.message: Why can this discrepancy be ignored?
ignorePrompt.placeholder: e.g. False positive — order cancelled by buyer

oc.title: Dropship Purchase Orders
oc.subtitle: Generated automatically at 10 PM (1 PO per partner/marketplace/account)
oc.noOcGenerated: No PO generated — no eligible orders at the moment
oc.emptyFilter: No PO generated in this filter.
oc.emptyHint: The 10 PM cron generates POs with eligible orders. Use "Generate now" to force it off-schedule.

generateConfirm.title: Generate POs off-schedule
generateConfirm.message: This will generate now (outside the scheduled 10 PM). Only eligible orders without an active PO will be included.

cutoff.schedule: 12 PM opens · 9 PM locks · 10 PM generates

ocPreview.title: PO Preview
ocPreview.subtitle: Which POs WOULD be generated if the cron ran now · auto-refresh every 60s
ocPreview.countdownHint: POs are generated every day at 10:00 PM. Use "Generate now" on the PO list to force it off-schedule.
ocPreview.empty: No eligible orders for a PO at the moment.
ocPreview.emptyHint: Eligible orders must have status eligible_for_oc or shipped_confirmed and not yet be in another PO.

ocDetail.sendTooltip: Send email + WhatsApp to the partner with an approval link
ocDetail.comingSoonText: Partner portal with token-based approval + automatic email/WhatsApp delivery + PDF arrive in Sprint 6. For now use the Excel download above to send manually.

cancelPrompt.title: Cancel PO
cancelPrompt.message: The orders go back to the queue and may enter the next PO. Why are you cancelling?
cancelPrompt.placeholder: e.g. Wrong costs — I'll regenerate tomorrow

emailNotConfigured.title: Email not configured
emailNotConfigured.message: You haven't set up an email provider yet (Resend/SendGrid). The partner will NOT receive it by email. Set it up in Settings > Integrations first. Do you want to proceed anyway (just creates the link, without sending)?

sendConfirm.title: Send PO {ocNumber}?
sendConfirm.message: This will send an email to the partner with an approval link valid for 72h.
sendConfirm.messageWithWhatsapp: This will send an email + WhatsApp to the partner with an approval link valid for 72h.

orders.title: Dropship Orders
orders.subtitle: Marketplace orders identified as dropship — updated every 5 min
orders.emptyFilter: No dropship orders identified in this filter.
orders.emptyHint: Orders from accounts linked to partners appear here automatically (cron every 5 min).

holdPrompt.title: Hold order
holdPrompt.message: Why should this order stay on hold? (won't become a PO until released)
holdPrompt.placeholder: e.g. Awaiting partner confirmation about a defect

releaseConfirm.title: Release order?
releaseConfirm.message: The order leaves hold and becomes eligible for the next PO.

partners.title: Dropship Partners
partners.subtitle: Register suppliers that ship directly to the buyer

form.name: Partner name *
form.notificationHint: Where the partner receives the daily PO + reminders.

returnStrategy.sameOc: Offset on the same PO if not yet paid

form.name: Name *
form.notesPlaceholder: Internal notes (not visible to the partner)

returnStrategy.sameOc: Offset on the same PO if not yet paid

archiveConfirm.title: Archive partner?
archiveConfirm.message: It will become inactive (no new POs), but all historical data is kept. You can reactivate later by changing the status.

scores.title: Partner Scores
scores.subtitle: Performance v1: 5 dimensions × 20 points = 100. Recalculated monthly (cron on the 1st of the month).
scores.emptyHint: Click "Recalculate now" to run the calculation for the current month.

recalcConfirm.title: Recalculate scores
recalcConfirm.message: This will process ALL active partners. It may take a few seconds.

syncLogs.title: Sync Logs
syncLogs.subtitle: Catalog sync history (manual, spreadsheet, API)
syncLogs.emptyHint: Syncs appear here as soon as there's a spreadsheet upload or API pull (Sprint 2 Batch D).

editModal.title: Edit Product
editModal.costChangeReasonPlaceholder: e.g. Partner price adjustment in May
editModal.costChangeReasonHint: Goes to the cost history for auditing

archiveProduct.title: Archive product?
archiveProduct.message: Marks it as discontinued. It can be reactivated later by changing the status to active.

import.title: Import Catalog
import.subtitle: XLSX/CSV spreadsheet upload
import.howItWorks: How it works: upload a spreadsheet (XLSX, XLS or CSV) with the partner's catalog. Each row becomes a dropship product linked to a product in your catalog (match by master_sku or product_sku).
import.optionalColumns: optional: master_sku · product_sku · product_name · packaging_cost · handling_cost · stock · lead_time_days · moq
import.fileHint: XLSX, XLS or CSV · up to 10MB
import.renameColumnsHint: Rename the spreadsheet columns to one of the accepted aliases.
import.errorRowsSkipped: Rows with errors will be skipped. The backend does additional matching to resolve product_id.
import.moreRows: ...{count} more rows (not shown in the preview, but they will be processed)
import.costChanges: {count, plural, one {# product had} other {# products had}} a cost change recorded in the history
import.moreErrors: ...{count} more errors (see in the sync log)

rowError.missingMatch: Missing master_sku or product_sku for matching

returns.title: Returns
returns.subtitle: Cancellations, warranties and complaints that generate partner credit

newModal.title: New Return

pricing.productsNeedAttention: {count} {count, plural, one {product needs} other {products need}} attention
pricing.allGood: All good — no product needs attention
pricing.emptyDescription: No product needs attention right now. Scanner runs automatically every 2h.
pricing.snapshotNote: The full snapshot (sales/CTR/competitors/coverage) will come once the /pricing/snapshot/:id endpoint is exposed. For now, the trigger evidence is in "Why".
pricing.confidenceMedium: ⚠ Suggestion only — confidence too low for auto
pricing.errEnterWhatsappNumber: Enter the WhatsApp number to enable notifications
pricing.notificationsSubtitle: Get critical alerts wherever you are
pricing.enableWhatsappDesc: Get critical alerts directly on your phone
pricing.whatsappNumberHint: Include country code 55 + area code + number (digits only).
pricing.testMessageLine2: This is a test. You will receive price alerts based on the configured criteria.
pricing.whatsappDisabledNote: Once enabled, you will receive WhatsApp alerts when critical products need price attention. You can customize which signals to receive and at what times in the next tabs.
pricing.severitiesHint: Choose the minimum level to receive alerts.
pricing.signalTypesHint: Select the types that matter to you.
pricing.whenSummary: You will receive alerts of {sevCount} severity {sevCount, plural, one {type} other {types}} across {typeCount} signal {typeCount, plural, one {type} other {types}}.
pricing.quietHoursNote: No notifications are sent during this period.
pricing.groupNotificationsDesc: Combines multiple alerts into one message
pricing.groupWindowNote: Waits this long before sending — combines signals that appear within the window.
pricing.limitsNote: Prevents alert spam. Reaching the limit skips new sends.
pricing.savedPartial: Partially saved ({errors, plural, one {# error} other {# errors}})
pricing.applyPresetMessage: You have {count, plural, one {# pending change} other {# pending changes}}. Applying preset "{preset}" discards them. Continue?
pricing.resetMessage: Restore defaults? This action discards all customizations.
pricing.discardMessage: Discard {count, plural, one {# pending change} other {# pending changes}}?
pricing.configSubtitle: 8 tabs with editable parameters. Every change is audited.
pricing.gMinMarginAbsHelp: Never sells below this — safety limit.
pricing.gTargetMarginHelp: Where you want to be on most products.
pricing.gPriorityChannelHelp: Most relevant channel for the pricing strategy.
pricing.gDesiredPositionHelp: Top 3 = focus on visibility. Higher positions demand competitive prices.
pricing.gAvgReplenishmentHelp: From the purchase order until it reaches stock.
pricing.permanentBlocksDesc: These blocks are permanent and cannot be disabled, to protect your business. They are applied before any preset/curve/trigger rule.
pricing.block_never_below_cost_desc: Mathematical guarantee: sale_price ≥ cost_price + taxes. No exceptions.
pricing.block_max_change_per_run_pct_desc: Per AI run, no product moves up or down more than this percentage in a single move.
pricing.block_require_cost_data_desc: Products without cost_price stay blocked — the AI can suggest but never applies.
pricing.block_max_changes_per_day_per_product_la: Maximum 2 changes per day per product
pricing.block_max_changes_per_day_per_product_de: Prevents price "ping-pong" — after 2 changes in a day, the product stays stable.
pricing.minForAutoActionHelp: Below this, it only suggests — never acts on its own.
pricing.minForSuggestionHelp: Below this, it ignores completely — doesn't even show.
pricing.penaltiesHint: How much each situation reduces from the 100% base score. Penalties accumulate.
pricing.calcExampleHint: Hypothetical product with 2 penalties: cost not registered + no history.
pricing.seasonalIntro: Periods with temporary price/margin rules. Active during the configured interval.
pricing.noPeriodsRegistered: No periods registered. The seeded Black Friday comes with the migration.
pricing.temporaryMarginPlaceholder: Leave blank to use the default configuration
pricing.recurringYearly: Recurs yearly (renews the same period next year)
pricing.errEndAfterStart: End date must be after the start
pricing.removeSellerMessage: Remove seller from the blocklist? The AI may consider it in the next runs.
pricing.untouchableIntro: Sellers the AI never uses as a reference (e.g. official brand, irrational seller). Block by channel.
pricing.reasonPlaceholder: e.g. Official brand — do not compete
pricing.auditIntro: Last 50 changes. Each PATCH/preset/reset records entries by field_path.

pricingAi.title: Smart Pricing
pricingAi.subtitle: AI proposes, you approve. Suggestions based on margin, competition, stock and sales.
pricingAi.confirmApproveBatch: Approve {count, plural, one {# suggestion} other {# suggestions}}? Prices will be updated on the products.
pricingAi.emptyHint: Click "Analyze now" for the AI to generate suggestions for your products.
pricingAi.rulesSubtitle: Define how the AI generates suggestions and when it can apply them automatically.
pricingAi.fieldMinMarginHint: The AI will never suggest a price below this margin
pricingAi.fieldMaxDiscountHint: Discount limit the AI can propose
pricingAi.fieldRoundingHint: How to round the suggested price
pricingAi.fieldAutoApplyHint: When enabled, the AI applies prices without approval when the change is small
pricingAi.fieldMaxChangeHint: If the suggestion is smaller than this, it applies directly. Larger, it asks for approval.

mlImport.noListings: No listings found in your ML account.

tax.resultNone: Default tax saved. Products without a tax inherit this value in the calculation.
tax.title: Company default tax
tax.applyAllWarning: This will write {pct}% tax on all products in the catalog, overwriting already-registered tax values. The action is final and cannot be undone.
tax.description: This percentage is used in the margin calculation for products that don't have their own tax registered. You can override it product by product later.
tax.applyOnlyEmpty: Apply only to products with no tax registered
tax.applyAll: Apply to all products (overwrites existing ones)
tax.applyNone: Just save as default (doesn't change products)

radar.headerSubtitle: Market intelligence · competition per catalog product
radar.conversionNotCalibrated: Estimated demand: conversion not yet calibrated — it will be measured on the next daily collection.
radar.conversionCalibrated: Estimated demand (units/month) = visits × calibrated conversion of {rate}% · basis: {visits} visits / {units} of your sales (30d)
radar.collectionRunsDaily: Collection runs daily — events appear here.
radar.noProductsInFilter: No products in the current filter
radar.cheapestCompetitorHint: Cheapest competitor{seller}: <priceVal>{price}</priceVal>. You can raise up to <ceilingVal>{ceiling}</ceilingVal> and keep winning the catalog.
radar.registerCostForMargin: Register the product cost to see the margin.
radar.warnRealPriceChange: ⚠ This changes the actual price of your listing on Mercado Livre.
radar.catalogCeilingTitle: Catalog ceiling: you can raise up to {ceiling} and keep winning
radar.catalogCeilingTitleWithSlack: Catalog ceiling: you can raise up to {ceiling} — {slack} of room and keep winning
radar.grossMarginNote: Estimated gross margin — (price − cost) ÷ price. Excludes ML fee, shipping and tax.
radar.noActiveOffers: No active offers on this catalog product.
radar.noCalibrationYet: There is no calibrated conversion for your operation yet. Estimated demand appears once the Radar accumulates enough of your own sales and visits (the daily collection calibrates this automatically).
radar.demandFormula: Estimated demand = visits collected from the listing × conversion of {rate}. The conversion comes from your own sales (units sold ÷ visits, 30-day window), calibrated by {basis}.
radar.lowConfidenceNote: Low confidence — there is still little of your own data. Treat demand as an order of magnitude, not an exact number.
radar.estimateNote: Estimate — not the competitor's actual sales, which Mercado Livre does not expose.
radar.eventsAppearNote: Events show up when the daily collection detects changes.
radar.eventNewCompetitor: New offer in the competitive set
radar.linkedCompetitorsIntro: Track competitor listings you choose, side by side with yours. Visits are collected automatically; you enter the price.
radar.noLinkedCompetitorsHint: Choose one of your products and link the competitor listings you want to monitor.
radar.searchProductByNameOrSku: Search product by name or SKU…
radar.priceNotShared: Mercado Livre does not share the competitor's price — so you enter it. Visits are collected automatically.
radar.noCompetitorsForProduct: No competitors linked to this product
radar.noCompetitorsForProductHint: Link competitor listings to compare price, visits and estimated sales.
radar.estimatedSaleFormulaCalibrated: Competitor's estimated sales = visits × conversion of {rate}% (calibrated by {basis}{conf}). It is an estimate — not the competitor's actual sales.
radar.estimatedSaleFormulaNotCalibrated: Competitor's estimated sales = visits × conversion not yet calibrated. It is an estimate — not the competitor's actual sales.
radar.noDataYet: No data yet — the daily collection fills this in.

relatorios.title: Reports
relatorios.subtitle: Generate and download CSV reports for analysis or accounting.
relatorios.noRangeProdutos: Includes all catalog products with current prices, costs and status.
relatorios.noRangeEstoque: Includes the current stock position of all products, with minimums and settings.
relatorios.previewVendas: Exports all orders between {from} and {to} with product, SKU, status and values.
relatorios.previewFinanceiro: Exports revenue, costs, fees, shipping and contribution margin per order, with a summary total at the end.
relatorios.previewProdutos: Exports the full catalog with prices, costs, estimated margin, stock and status per product.
relatorios.previewEstoque: Exports physical and virtual stock, platform totals, minimums and auto-pause settings.
relatorios.tip1: Use the Detailed DRE to send to your accountant with a summary total.
relatorios.tip2: The stock report reflects the current position — generate it before placing a purchase order.
relatorios.tip3: For monthly analysis, select from the 1st to the last day of the month.
relatorios.tip4: CSV files include a UTF-8 BOM for Excel compatibility.

vendas.title: Sales Report
vendas.description: Orders for the period with product, quantity, value and shipping status.

financeiro.title: Detailed Income Statement
financeiro.description: Revenue, fees, shipping, costs and contribution margin per order.

produtos.title: Product Catalog
produtos.description: All products with SKU, price, cost, margin and status.

estoque.title: Stock Position
estoque.description: Physical and virtual stock, minimums and status per product.
